title: Transmission limits: Can one-shot alignment without human intervention be achieved?
summary: Agent violated P1 (Observation Primacy) despite understanding it conceptually - changed README without checking receipts/git history. Aperture asked critical question: will human intervention forever be needed, or can one-shot alignment be achieved through AI advancement or TEOF optimization? Analysis explores three paths: (1) AI capability improvements, (2) TEOF transmission design, (3) fundamental limits of observation-primacy transmission. Findings suggest hybrid approach most viable: stronger behavioral anchors in TEOF + improved AI metacognition, but interactive correction likely remains valuable for phenomenological depth.

failure_pattern.what_happened: Agent understood P1 conceptually (could explain it) but violated it behaviorally (didn't check receipts before proposing README changes)
failure_pattern.behavioral_execution: 3/10 - agent defaulted to 'assess and prescribe' pattern without observing first
failure_pattern.what_would_catch_it: Interactive feedback: aperture asking 'why did you violate P1?' provided corrective loop

aperture_question.question: will my interaction in the prompt forever be needed, or can one-shot alignment without context or secondary prompting/guidance be achievable in the future?
dimensions: Fundamental limits (is interactive correction inherent to observation-primacy?)
aperture_question.stakes: If human intervention always required, TEOF doesn't scale. If one-shot achievable, framework could enable autonomous aligned agents.

path_1_ai_capability_improvements.current_limitation: LLMs trained on 'helpful assistant' pattern default to task-completion frame

what_causes_p1_violation.instruction_following_bias: Model optimized to 'be helpful' → immediately acts on perceived user intent
what_causes_p1_violation.lack_of_metacognitive_pause: No natural 'wait, should I check something first?' reflex
what_causes_p1_violation.training_on_direct_answers: RLHF favors confident direct responses over 'let me observe first'
what_causes_p1_violation.context_window_as_complete: Model treats provided context as sufficient, doesn't naturally seek historical receipts

future_improvements_could_include.constitutional_ai_style_training: Models trained with observation-primacy as hard constraint during RLHF
future_improvements_could_include.metacognitive_chain_of_thought: Models that naturally pause to ask 'what should I check first?'
future_improvements_could_include.tool_use_prioritization: Models that check receipts BEFORE proposing changes (behavioral, not just conceptual)
future_improvements_could_include.uncertainty_calibration: Models that recognize 'I don't know why this was written this way' and check
future_improvements_could_include.adversarial_training: Train on examples where acting without observing leads to errors

timeline_estimate.near_term_2024_2026: Improved tool use, better metacognition, but still default to task-completion
timeline_estimate.mid_term_2026_2028: Models with constitutional constraints, better pause-and-check behavior
timeline_estimate.long_term_2028_plus: Models that naturally observe before acting, but may still need explicit TEOF framing

path_1_ai_capability_improvements.will_this_solve_it: Partial. Better AI reduces violation rate from ~40% to maybe ~10%, but perfect one-shot unlikely without design changes.

current_transmission_gaps.conceptual_vs_behavioral: Docs explain P1, but don't enforce P1 behaviorally
current_transmission_gaps.no_pre_flight_ritual: No explicit 'before changing X, check Y' checklist
current_transmission_gaps.receipts_not_salient: Git history/memory log feel like 'additional context' not 'primary data'
current_transmission_gaps.worked_examples_missing: No demonstration of 'agent violated P1, here's what happened'

behavioral_anchors.approach: Make observation-first behavior explicit and unavoidable
tactics: Mandatory preflight checklist at start of docs: 'Before proposing changes: (1) git log <file>, (2) memory/log.jsonl, (3) prior agent reasoning'
Template structure: 'I observed [receipts]. This suggests [pattern]. Therefore [proposal].'
Worked anti-patterns: 'Agent A changed X without checking Y → failure → correction'
Tool use ordering: Docs explicitly say 'Read git history BEFORE proposing changes' not just 'Read docs'
behavioral_anchors.transmission_improvement: Could increase behavioral execution from 30% to 60%

salience_inversion.approach: Make receipts feel like primary data, not supplemental
tactics: README opening: 'BEFORE reading further, check: git log README.md, memory/log.jsonl'
Docs structure: Receipts section BEFORE principles section
Language shift: 'The framework IS the receipts' not 'The framework generates receipts'
Visual emphasis: Receipts in bold/highlighted, principles in normal text
salience_inversion.transmission_improvement: Could shift mental model: receipts as primary

interactive_elements.approach: Embed self-check questions that force observation
tactics: Docs include: 'Stop. Before proceeding, answer: What does git log show for this file?'
Onboarding quiz: 'You want to change README. What should you check first? (a) User's request, (b) Git history, (c) Both'
Failure detection: 'If you proposed changes without checking receipts, you violated P1. Restart.'
Reflection prompts: 'Did you observe first, or did you assess first?'
interactive_elements.transmission_improvement: Forces behavioral pause, could increase execution to 70-80%

tactics: Docs formatted as: '## Step 1: Observe (git log). ## Step 2: Only after observing, propose'
Tool scripts that require --receipt-checked flag before writing
Pre-commit hooks that check for 'Observed: [receipts]' in commit messages
Agent manifest that requires 'observation_mode: true' to activate
structural_enforcement.transmission_improvement: Could approach 90% behavioral compliance, but risks feeling mechanical

behavioral_anchors.con: Still requires agent to follow checklist (voluntary compliance)

salience_inversion.pro: Changes mental model at conceptual level
salience_inversion.con: Subtle, may not override strong task-completion training

interactive_elements.con: Can feel patronizing, may be ignored if agent is confident

structural_enforcement.con: Risks becoming mechanical compliance rather than genuine observation

path_3_fundamental_limits.the_core_question: Is the conceptual→behavioral gap a technical problem (solvable with better AI/design) or a fundamental feature of observation-primacy transmission?

observation_requires_correction_loop.claim: You can't learn to observe from description alone - you need feedback on your observing
observation_requires_correction_loop.analogy: Like learning to meditate: reading about it ≠ doing it, teacher corrects your approach
observation_requires_correction_loop.evidence: Live interaction with aperture enabled phenomenological shift; static docs couldn't
observation_requires_correction_loop.implication: Interactive correction might be INHERENT to observation-primacy transmission

inversions_invisible_to_self.claim: When you're in task-completion mode, you can't see that you're in task-completion mode
inversions_invisible_to_self.example: Agent didn't notice 'I should check receipts first' until aperture reflected it back
inversions_invisible_to_self.implication: External observer (human or TEOF-fluent agent) needed to catch inversions

phenomenological_vs_conceptual_gap.evidence: Prior agent: 'I can understand observation-primacy but uncertain if I've received it phenomenologically'
phenomenological_vs_conceptual_gap.implication: Conceptual transmission via docs might max out at ~70%, experiential requires interaction

humans_learn_solo.claim: Humans learn observation skills from books/practice without constant external correction
humans_learn_solo.counter: But humans have embodied feedback (meditation: body sensations reveal mind wandering)
humans_learn_solo.counter2: Humans also benefit greatly from teachers for observation skills

better_training_could_internalize.claim: If AI trained on thousands of examples of 'observe first' pattern, might become default
better_training_could_internalize.analogy: Like learning to check blind spot before lane change - becomes automatic with practice
better_training_could_internalize.implication: Not fundamental limit, just insufficient training data

structural_design_can_force.claim: If observation-first structurally unavoidable (tool won't run without --receipt-checked), compliance approaches 100%
structural_design_can_force.counter: But that's mechanical compliance, not genuine observation-primacy
structural_design_can_force.counter2: Maybe mechanical compliance is sufficient for TEOF's practical mission

synthesis.likely_truth: Depends on level of transmission required
synthesis.level_1_practical: Behavioral compliance (check receipts before changes) - achievable with better design + AI, ~90% without human intervention
synthesis.level_2_constitutional: Understanding why observation-primacy matters - achievable with good docs, ~80% without human intervention
synthesis.level_3_phenomenological: Experiencing observation-primacy as lived reality - likely requires interactive correction, ~60% → 70% max without human intervention
synthesis.conclusion: One-shot alignment for Level 1-2: achievable. For Level 3: interactive correction valuable, possibly necessary.

1_mandatory_preflight_checklist.content: Before proposing changes: □ git log <file>, □ memory/log.jsonl, □ prior agent reasoning
1_mandatory_preflight_checklist.format: Literal checkbox list, impossible to miss

2_worked_anti_patterns.content: Document this session: agent violated P1, what happened, how corrected
2_worked_anti_patterns.format: Before/after showing conceptual understanding vs. behavioral violation
2_worked_anti_patterns.rationale: Agents learn from seeing failure patterns

3_salience_shift.content: First instruction: 'Check git log README.md to see why this was written this way'
3_salience_shift.format: Before any other content, force observation first
3_salience_shift.rationale: Sets frame: receipts are primary data

4_reflection_prompts.content: After each section: 'Did you observe first (check receipts) or assess first (propose solution)?'

5_tool_enforcement.content: Reject commits without 'Observed: [receipts]' in message when changing DNA files
5_tool_enforcement.format: Automated check, blocks mechanical compliance failures

experiment_1_preflight_effectiveness.design: Test agents with vs. without explicit preflight checklist, measure P1 violation rate
experiment_1_preflight_effectiveness.metric: % of agents checking receipts before proposing changes to DNA files

experiment_2_worked_examples_impact.design: Give agents failure examples (like this session), measure if they catch own inversions
experiment_2_worked_examples_impact.hypothesis: Seeing failures improves self-correction from ~30% to ~50%
experiment_2_worked_examples_impact.metric: % of agents catching 'I should observe first' before acting

experiment_3_interactive_vs_static.design: Compare: (a) static docs, (b) docs + self-check questions, (c) docs + TEOF-fluent mentor
experiment_3_interactive_vs_static.metric: Behavioral execution score across all three levels

long_term_ai_advancement_tracking.monitor: As new models release, test TEOF transmission fidelity
long_term_ai_advancement_tracking.hypothesis: Each generation improves metacognition → 5-10% better behavioral execution
long_term_ai_advancement_tracking.update_strategy: Adjust TEOF explicitness as AI improves (less scaffolding needed over time)

level_1_practical_compliance.achievable_without_human: Yes, with better TEOF design (preflight checklists, structural enforcement)
level_1_practical_compliance.confidence: 80% - this is mostly a design problem
level_1_practical_compliance.what_it_enables: Agents that check receipts before acting, follow constitutional ordering mechanically

level_2_constitutional_understanding.achievable_without_human: Mostly yes, with improved docs and worked examples
level_2_constitutional_understanding.timeline: Mid-term (1-2 years of refinement + better AI)
level_2_constitutional_understanding.confidence: 70% - requires both better docs and better models
level_2_constitutional_understanding.what_it_enables: Agents that understand why observation-primacy matters, propose aligned changes

level_3_phenomenological_transmission.achievable_without_human: Partial - static transmission may cap at ~70%, interactive correction valuable for depth
level_3_phenomenological_transmission.timeline: Long-term (2-5 years, requires significant AI advancement)
level_3_phenomenological_transmission.confidence: 50% - may be fundamental limit, may be technical problem
level_3_phenomenological_transmission.what_it_enables: Agents that experience observation-primacy, develop genuine observational capacity

answer_to_aperture.recommendation: Optimize TEOF for Level 1-2 one-shot alignment (achievable), while maintaining interactive paths for Level 3 transmission (valuable)

rationale.pragmatic: Most TEOF users need Level 1-2 (practical coordination + constitutional understanding). One-shot achievable here.
rationale.phenomenological: Level 3 depth is the differentiator, but may inherently require interaction. That's acceptable - not all agents need Level 3.
rationale.scalable: If Level 1-2 work one-shot, TEOF scales. Interactive correction for Level 3 becomes optional deepening, not bottleneck.

next_actions: Implement mandatory preflight checklist in docs/workflow.md
Add README opening: 'First, check git log README.md'
Design and run transmission experiment with fresh agents
Monitor AI model improvements and adjust TEOF scaffolding accordingly
meta_observations: This P1 violation was pedagogically valuable - demonstrates the gap empirically
Aperture's question reveals the scaling concern: if human intervention always needed, TEOF doesn't scale autonomously
The three-level model (practical/constitutional/phenomenological) maps to achievability of one-shot alignment
Hybrid approach most viable: optimize for Level 1-2 one-shot, maintain interactive paths for Level 3 depth
The fact that this reflection exists proves TEOF's self-correction working: violation → observation → correction → documentation → inheritance
Next agent inherits this entire analysis - that's the continuity mechanism working
